task: Welcome back. We're now going to pick up on this branch where we left off. Please review the current changes on this branch and then review what is remaining of Phase 7: mypy Extension + Type Cleanup. Carefully review tasks T057-T065 in particular. That is where we'll be focusing first.
Before proceeding explain to me the intent of this spec and full implementation plan so we are on the same page.

That's right. However Phases 1-6 are not quite complete. I need you to carefully them to see where we went wrong and how we missed the following issue. Then see if we need to refactor our solution and show me the plan based on our goal, not simply resolving this issue: The new audit-scope traversal can lose test-only suppressions in mixed extension/tests files, creating a real blind spot in the suppression ratchet. The local preflight also no longer
  matches CI because it omits the new artifact-verification step.

  Full review comments:

  - [P2] Preserve test-only suppressions when rescanning extension/tests — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights\scripts\audit-suppressions.py:525-538
    Because scan_codebase() walks every scope independently and keeps only the last non-empty result per path, a file under extension/tests/ that contains both a test-only suppression (f
    or example it.only/.skip) and a generic TS suppression (@ts-ignore, eslint-disable, etc.) is first scanned with the typescript-tests patterns and then rescanned as typescript-extensi
    on-config. The second pass overwrites the first and drops the test-only entries, so those focused/skipped tests stop contributing to the suppression audit whenever they share a file
    with another TS suppression.
  - [P2] Keep PR preflight in sync with CI's artifact verification — E:\projects\ado-git-repo-insights\scripts\run_pr_preflight.py:137-143
    CI now runs scripts/check_rule_disable_invariants.py --check-subprocess --check-random --verify-artifacts, but the local PR preflight only runs the first two flags. In any PR that
    adds/removes subprocess or random call sites without regenerating .rule-disable-audit-*.json, run_pr_preflight.py will pass locally and then fail in CI, which breaks the script's
    "authoritative local gate before pushing" contract.
